name: Armagnac Prune Cake
recipeInstructions:
FOR CAKE: Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 350°F. Butter and flour a 12-cup bundt pan. Combine prunes, water, and Armagnac in a heavy medium saucepan. Simmer until prunes are tender, about 15 minutes. Drain prunes, reserving 1/4 cup cooking liquid for glaze. Coarsely chop prunes.
Beat oil, sugar, eggs, and vanilla in a large bowl until well blended. In a medium bowl, mix flour, baking soda, spices, and salt to blend. Mix dry ingredients into the wet mixture. Add buttermilk, beating until batter is smooth. Fold in chopped prunes. Transfer batter to prepared pan. Bake cake until a tester comes out clean, about 1 hour 5 minutes. Transfer to rack in pan.
FOR GLAZE: Combine reserved 1/4 cup prune cooking liquid, sugar, butter, Armagnac, corn syrup, lemon juice, and baking soda in a heavy large saucepan. Bring to boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Boil for 2 minutes. Pierce cake in several places with a long toothpick or wooden skewer. Slowly pour about 1-1/4 cups glaze over hot cake. Reserve extra glaze. Let glazed cake cool in pan for 30 minutes. Turn out onto a platter. Cool completely. Cut into wedges. Serve with vanilla ice cream, passing additional glaze separately as sauce.
Bon Appetit - April 1991
